title: TrueLight FX Lite
description: A lightweight Shader for low end Hardware
body:
## Showcase/Tutorial:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/uxd1QZJdpGw" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Huge thanks to [@Titanaraa](https://www.youtube.com/@Titanaraa) for creating this awesome video! 

# TrueLightFX Lite


- Looking like Vibrant-visuals without the FPS cost. A lightweight shader built for low to mid-range PCs that refuses to look boring.

## What You Get:

### -     Realistic Waves - animated water surface with adjustable size and speed
### -     God Rays - light shafts through trees and windows, with intensity control
### -     Dynamic Fog - golden daylight tones, naturally dark nights and caves
### -     Glowing Ores - real additive emission that cuts through cave darkness
### -     Underwater Caustics - animated light patterns via fast sine-wave math
### -     Wavy Plants - wind-affected leaves, sand, and vegetation
### -     Bloom - adjustable post-processing glow
### -     TAA + FXAA - your choice of anti-aliasing
### -     Two Cloud Styles - Standard or Enhanced
### -     Smart Profiles - instant presets from "low" to "your pc isnt that slow isnt it".         


Credits to Ukrech for the [Source Project](https://github.com/mateuskreuch/minecraft-miniature-shader).


Compatibility:
TrueLightFX might work with the Minecraft versions 1.8.2 – 1.17, but older versions may experience bugs and reduced smoothness. For the best experience, it is strongly recommended to use Minecraft 1.17 or newer.Also I dont recomment using optifine bc of some features that are very buggy on Optfine but on Iris they just work fine!
